title: rosa canina f. stoderana
intro: Rosa canina f. stoderana, also known as the dwarf dog rose, is a variety of the Rosa canina species, a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina f. stoderana lie in its hips, which are rich in vitamins A and C, as well as flavonoids and carotenoids, making it a valuable source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rosa canina f. stoderana include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Rosa canina f. stoderana can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
